headline: Labuan Marine Park Coral Cover Falls to 18% from 52% in 2010
context: The Labuan Marine Park Authority's 2025 reef survey recorded 18% live coral cover, down from 52% in 2010, attributed to sedimentation from mainland development and anchoring damage.

cards.big: Labuan Marine Park was gazetted in 1994 to protect coral reefs.
cards.sub: 31 years later, coral cover has dropped from 52% to 18%. The park exists on paper; the reef barely exists underwater.

cards.big: Mooring buoys were installed in 2012 to prevent anchor damage.
cards.sub: Only 8 of 24 buoys remain functional in 2025. Maintenance budget: RM0 since 2020.

cards.big: Sedimentation from Labuan's development increased turbidity by 340%.
cards.sub: Corals need light. At current turbidity, photosynthesis is inhibited below 3 metres.

cards.big: Dive tourism at Labuan dropped from 34,000 visitors in 2015 to 11,000 in 2024.
cards.sub: Revenue decline of RM18M annually — more than the total cost of reef rehabilitation.

cards.big: A marine park with no maintenance budget and 18% coral cover is a marine park in name only.
Marine protection requires ongoing funding, not just gazette notices. Labuan shows what happens when we declare victory and walk away.
